element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player defines 7 primary statistics, 18 skills, 2 traits before entering world
Safe underground refuge; establishes baseline before wasteland exposure
NPCs provide quests, dialogue options vary; some solutions require diplomacy
150 in-game day time limit; failure triggers game end
Escalates through wasteland exploration; multiple tactical approaches
Trimetric perspective navigation; player-driven pacing and routing
AI-controlled allies; cannot be directly commanded
Resource management for equipment and supplies
Unlocked progressively; 48 perks with prerequisites; augments combat and utility
Quest solutions gain/lose reputation; affects NPC treatment and dialogue
Quest tracking and inventory management tool

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to safe zone (vault or settlement)
typical_tension_triggers: 150 in-game day time limit pressure on main quest
Morally ambiguous quest choices with unknown consequences
Reputation system penalties from quest resolution
Unpredictable NPC hostility or faction conflicts
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Player-driven with hard time limit constraint; tension oscillates between safe dialogue/commerce moments and dangerous wasteland exploration; pacing allows deliberate planning or rushed completion strategies

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Player freedom in exploration order; side quests are optional; multiple NPCs and factions suggest branching area discovery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Vault introduction establishes baseline character; initial wasteland encounters introduce turn-based combat with low-tier threats
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation through expanded wasteland exploration; tougher enemies; increased quest complexity with moral weight; perk system customization becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-stakes faction interactions; complex quest chains with cascading reputation consequences; approaching 150-day deadline pressure intensifies
notable_spikes: First vault exit to hostile wasteland environment
Faction warfare conflicts affecting quest availability
Final main quest chain with deadline proximity

notable_level_patterns.description: Safe settlement/vault acts as hub; wasteland radiates outward with discoverable locations, NPCs, quests; player returns for resource replenishment and dialogue
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Vault 13 to surrounding Shady Sands, Junktown

notable_level_patterns.description: Early quests offer simple fetch/dialogue solutions; mid-game quests require choosing between conflicting factions or moral compromise; late-game quests cascade with reputation consequences
Most quests support diplomacy, combat, stealth, or unconventional approaches; player agency determines completion method and consequence severity
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple quest archetypes repeatable across locations

notable_level_patterns.description: 150 in-game day main quest deadline creates meta-rhythm; player can ignore and explore freely, or rush; failure triggers game end, forcing pacing awareness
